name: Lisbon
localTips: Wear real shoes — Lisbon's calçada cobbles are beautiful, steep and lethally slippery in rain or flip-flops.
The 'couvert' (bread, olives, cheese) isn't free — if you touch it, you pay for it. Wave it away if you don't want it.
Buy a Viva Viagem card and load it with 'zapping' credit — it works on trams, metro, ferries and even the Santa Justa lift.
Tram 28 is packed with pickpockets by mid-morning. Ride at 7am, or take tram 12 for the same views without the crush.
A 'bica' is a Lisbon espresso — order it standing at the counter with a pastel de nata, pay under €3.

activities.title: Alfama's tangled lanes
activities.description: The Moorish old quarter tumbles downhill in a knot of stairways, tiled façades and laundry lines — aim for the São Jorge castle walls and get lost on purpose.

activities.title: Miradouro (viewpoint) crawl
activities.description: Link Portas do Sol, Graça and Senhora do Monte — each terrace stacks orange rooftops against the Tejo, and most have a kiosk pouring cold drinks.

activities.title: Ribeira das Naus riverfront
activities.description: Lisbon's 'riverside beach' — stone steps into the Tejo between Cais do Sodré and Praça do Comércio, where the whole city comes to watch the light.

activities.title: Belém monuments walk
activities.description: The Discoveries Monument, Belém Tower and the Jerónimos façade along one riverside kilometre — the Age of Exploration in stone.

activities.title: LX Factory creative quarter
activities.description: A 19th-century industrial complex under the bridge, reborn as bookshops, murals, rooftop bars and Sunday markets — Lisbon's creative living room.

activities.title: Time Out Market food hall
activities.description: Twenty-six of Lisbon's best kitchens under one iron roof at Cais do Sodré — from €3 croquetes to Michelin-starred counters.

activities.title: Sé Cathedral & Graça churches
activities.description: Lisbon's fortress-like 12th-century cathedral, then uphill to São Vicente de Fora's tiled cloisters — Romanesque to azulejo in one climb.

activities.title: Jardim da Estrela & basilica
activities.description: A romantic 19th-century garden of banyans and bandstands opposite the white dome of the Estrela Basilica — the locals' favorite picnic lawn.

activities.title: Baixa to Chiado stroll
activities.description: Pombaline grid streets, the Santa Justa lift's iron lattice, A Brasileira's café terrace and the world's oldest bookshop (Bertrand, 1732).

activities.title: Pink Street & Cais do Sodré by night
activities.description: The old red-light strip painted pink and reborn as Lisbon's bar row — start under the umbrellas of Rua Nova do Carvalho and follow the noise.

activities.title: Azulejo façade hunt
activities.description: Lisbon's tiles turn every street into a gallery — hunt the best façades along Rua do Possolo, Campo de Ourique and the Alfama backstreets.

activities.title: Campo de Ourique market
activities.description: The neighborhood version of Time Out Market — same great petiscos and wine counters, half the prices, and actual Lisboetas at the tables.

activities.title: Príncipe Real garden & boutiques
activities.description: An octopus-like century-old cedar shades the square; around it, Lisbon's best concept stores fill 19th-century palacetes.

activities.title: Tram 28 at dawn
activities.description: The wooden remodelado tram rattles through Graça, Alfama and Estrela — ride the whole line at 7am when it's just you and commuters.
activities.priceHint: €3.10 on board, less with Viva card
activities.neighborhood: Martim Moniz to Campo de Ourique

activities.title: Ginjinha at the hole-in-the-wall bars
activities.description: Sour-cherry liqueur served in a shot glass from century-old counters — A Ginjinha on Largo de São Domingos has poured little else since 1840.
activities.insiderNote: Order 'com elas' (with the cherries in) and drink it standing on the square like everyone else. €1.50 well spent.

activities.title: Fado in a Mouraria tasca
activities.description: Skip the €50 dinner shows — in Mouraria's tiny tascas like Tasca do Chico, fado is sung between tables for the price of a glass of wine.
activities.insiderNote: 'Fado vadio' nights are open-mic — anyone from grandmothers to off-duty pros may stand up. Silence during songs is sacred.

activities.title: The pastel de nata taste-off
activities.description: Pastéis de Belém (the 1837 original, secret recipe) versus Manteigaria in Chiado — eat both warm with cinnamon and pick your side.
activities.insiderNote: At Belém, skip the takeaway queue and sit in the tiled back rooms — table service is faster. Manteigaria rings a bell when a fresh batch emerges.

activities.title: Feira da Ladra flea market
activities.description: The 'thieves' market' behind São Vicente — azulejo fragments, vinyl, brass and junk since the 13th century, Tuesdays and Saturdays.
activities.insiderNote: Haggle gently, bring cash, and go before 11:00 for the good tiles. The Panteão Nacional next door has a rooftop few visitors notice.

activities.title: Ferry to Cacilhas for seafood & the view back
activities.description: Ten minutes across the Tejo (€1.55) to riverside seafood halls and the best panorama of Lisbon's amphitheatre of hills.
activities.insiderNote: Walk left along the derelict riverfront to Ponto Final — the yellow-table restaurant on the water. Book or arrive at opening.

activities.title: Ler Devagar bookshop & rainy-day LX
activities.description: A bookshop in an old printing hall with a flying bicycle sculpture and presses still in place — the anchor of LX Factory's covered corners.
activities.insiderNote: Upstairs mezzanine has café tables among the stacks — one of the city's best rainy-afternoon hideouts.

activities.title: Quiosque culture: drinks at the kiosks
activities.description: Lisbon's Art Nouveau kiosks pour wine, coffee and toasted sandwiches on leafy squares — the Príncipe Real and Jardim da Estrela ones are institutions.
activities.insiderNote: A glass of vinho verde at a kiosk runs €2.50–3.50 — cheaper than any bar, with better people-watching.

activities.title: Jerónimos Monastery cloisters
activities.description: Manueline stone carved like rope and coral around a two-storey cloister — Portugal's golden age in one building. Vasco da Gama rests inside.

activities.title: Oceanário de Lisboa
activities.description: One of the world's great aquariums — a colossal central tank where sharks, rays and the resident sunfish circle past four ocean habitats.

activities.title: Sintra & Pena Palace day trip
activities.description: A fairy-tale palace in circus colors on a misty mountaintop, Moorish castle walls and the Initiation Well at Quinta da Regaleira — 40 minutes by train.
activities.bookingQuery: Sintra Pena Palace Regaleira day trip from Lisbon

activities.title: Tejo sunset sailing cruise
activities.description: Glide under the 25 de Abril bridge past Belém as the city turns gold — two hours on the water with a glass of wine included.
activities.bookingQuery: sunset sailing cruise Tagus river Lisbon

activities.title: Electric tuk-tuk hills tour
activities.description: Let someone else handle the gradients — a 90-minute loop through Alfama, Graça and the miradouros with photo stops and local commentary.
activities.bookingQuery: electric tuk tuk tour Alfama viewpoints Lisbon

activities.title: Mouraria & Alfama food tour
activities.description: Bifanas, ginjinha, cheeses and petiscos across the old quarters with a guide who greets half the shopkeepers by name.
activities.bookingQuery: Lisbon food and wine walking tour Mouraria Alfama

activities.title: Professional fado show with dinner
activities.description: The full velvet-curtain experience in a historic Alfama fado house — three singers, two guitarists and Portuguese classics over dinner.
activities.bookingQuery: fado show with dinner Alfama Lisbon

activities.title: Paint-your-own azulejo workshop
activities.description: Learn the five-century-old tile craft in a working atelier and fire your own azulejo to take home — the souvenir nobody else has.

activities.title: Belém Tower entry
activities.description: Climb the spiral stair of the 1519 river fortress — rhinoceros gargoyle included — for ramparts views over the Tejo.

activities.title: Santo António street festivals
activities.description: All June, Alfama and Graça hang paper garlands and grill sardines in the streets — the night of June 12th is the city's biggest party.
